children.heading: Initial designation
children.label: a Initial designation
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Department of State shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees and the Committees on Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives a report, in classified form, that contains a list of diplomatic and consular posts designated as high risk, high threat posts.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Designations before opening or reopening posts
children.label: b Designations before opening or reopening posts
children.text: Before opening or reopening a diplomatic or consular post, the Secretary shall determine if such post should be designated as a high risk, high threat post.

children.heading: Designating existing posts
children.label: c Designating existing posts
children.text: The Secretary shall regularly review existing diplomatic and consular posts to determine if any such post should be designated as a high risk, high threat post if conditions at such post or the surrounding security environment require such a designation.

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 1 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: with high to critical levels of political violence and terrorism; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the government of which lacks the ability or willingness to provide adequate security; and

children.label: B
children.text: has mission physical security platforms that fall below the Department of State’s established standards.
